citations.desc: Cleaning, sanitization, housekeeping, and pest control.
citations.narrative:
There is an excessive accumulation of excreta in the bird enclosure housing 5 blue and gold macaws.
There is a wood perch running diagonally next to the feed pan that has a pile of dried fecal material measuring
approximately 5 inches in length by about 3 inches in height. This pile is located about halfway down the perch. The
bottom half of that same wood perch also contains an accumulation of dried fecal material. There is a viewing window for
the public that is a part of the enclosure. The bottom rim of that window has an accumulation of fecal material running the
entire length.
Failure to clean accumulations of waste materials can increase disease hazards, attract insects, pests, and be a source of
odors.
Excreta and food waste must be removed from the primary enclosures and from under and from around primary
enclosures as often as necessary to prevent excessive accumulation of feces and food waste, to reduce disease hazards,
insects, pests, and odors.
Correct by 3/23/2026
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a facility representative.
n

narrative:
3.158(a)(1) 
   
Cleaning, sanitization, housekeeping, and pest control. 
There is an excessive accumulation of excreta in the bird enclosure housing 5 blue and gold macaws.  
 
There is a wood perch running diagonally next to the feed pan that has a pile of dried fecal material measuring 
approximately 5 inches in length by about 3 inches in height. This pile is located about halfway down the perch. The 
bottom half of that same wood perch also contains an accumulation of dried fecal material. There is a viewing window for 
the public that is a part of the enclosure. The bottom rim of that window has an accumulation of fecal material running the 
entire length. 
 
Failure to clean accumulations of waste materials can increase disease hazards, attract insects, pests, and be a source of 
odors. 
 
Excreta and food waste must be removed from the primary enclosures and from under and from around primary 
enclosures as often as necessary to prevent excessive accumulation of feces and food waste, to reduce disease hazards, 
insects, pests, and odors. 
Correct by 3/23/2026 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a facility representative.
